title: Dactylorhiza incarnata lusus albida
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata lusus albida, also known as the White-flowered Marsh Orchid, is a rare and geographically limited variant of the Common Spotted Orchid (Dactylorhiza incarnata), a perennial orchid native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza incarnata lusus albida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dactylorhiza incarnata lusus albida is commonly prepared as a tincture, dried extract, or decoction, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza incarnata lusus albida is a variant of the species Dactylorhiza incarnata, which is a type of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Dactylorhiza. The species Dactylorhiza is further classified within the subgenus Dactylorhiza, and the genus is a member of the tribe Orchideae. The Orchideae tribe is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. This classification helps to group and identify Dactylorhiza incarnata lusus albida within the broader plant kingdom.
